title: Your First Node.js Program

slides.title: Hello, World! đź‘‹
slides.content:
# Your First Script

Create a new file called `hello.js` with the following content:

```javascript
console.log("Hello, World!");
```

Now open your terminal, navigate to the folder containing `hello.js`, and run:

```bash
node hello.js
```

You should see:
```
Hello, World!
```

Congratulations â€“ you've just run a Node.js program!

slides.title: Try it Yourself
slides.content: Write the code and run it:

quizData.question: What is the output of `console.log("Hi");`?
quizData.explanation: `console.log()` prints its argument to the terminal. The quotes are not part of the output.

slides.title: Passing Arguments to Your Script đź“Ą
slides.content:
## Using `process.argv`

Node gives you access to commandâ€‘line arguments via `process.argv`. It's an array where:
- First element: path to Node executable
- Second element: path to your script file
- Remaining elements: arguments you passed

Example `greet.js`:
```javascript
const args = process.argv.slice(2);
const name = args[0] || 'World';
console.log(`Hello, ${name}!`);
```

Run: `node greet.js Node` â†’ Output: `Hello, Node!`
